mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand and construction activity, which jointly suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient economic output that offsets higher discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods yield negative or flat 20-day forward returns in greater than 50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend signals expanding economic activity that supports corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 revisions correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 55% of cases over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly are followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness that undermines consumer spending power, driving negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces a positive rank IC in greater than 70% of 20-day forward return predictions.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal income growth during a post-shock recovery phase reflects restored household purchasing power that immediately boosts equity risk appetite, generating positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 over rolling 3-year samples.
